topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the soft thud of a dropped book. I clicked it open, the subject line, "Party Update," seeming to mock me. My eyes scanned the text, the familiar warmth of anticipation draining away with each sentence. "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise celebration scheduled for Saturday has been cancelled." I reread the words. Unforeseen circumstances. My stomach tightened into a cold, hard knot. I sat back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight. I looked around my apartment, at the half-inflated balloons still tethered to the wall, and felt a strange lightness, as if the air had been sucked out of the room. I picked up my phone, my fingers hovering over the contact for Sarah, a slow, shaky breath escaping my lips.

[story 2]

It wasn't until I saw the hastily scrawled note taped to the refrigerator that the reality hit. The silver foil, usually gleaming with promise, now felt dull and mocking. My hands trembled as I peeled the paper off. The handwriting was familiar, but the message itself was a blunt instrument. *The surprise is off.* I stood motionless in the kitchen, the silence of the apartment amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent streetlights, trying to process the sudden void where excitement used to reside. A strange, hollow ache settled behind my ribs. I let out a slow, ragged sigh, the sound catching in my throat, and slowly turned back to the kitchen, the world feeling suddenly tilted on its axis.

[story 3]

I kept refreshing my messages, expecting a retraction, a typo, anything to suggest this was a mistake. Each new notification was just a confirmation of the emptiness. When I finally read the official announcement, the noise of the world seemed to mute itself. My jaw went slack. I dropped the remote control onto the sofa cushion; it made a dull, insignificant sound. I stood up and walked to the hallway mirror, examining my own face. My expression was unreadable, a blank canvas reflecting the sudden deflation inside me. I didn't cry, but a profound stillness took over. I just stood there, absorbing the absolute lack of expected joy, feeling profoundly unbalanced, as if gravity had suddenly shifted beneath my feet.
